payload.title: Define narada resume as inhabited continuity recovery
payload.summary: Operator and architect identified a strong conceptual meaning for a narada resume command: resume an inhabited thread of work from durable traces. The command should recover the appropriate locus, active arc/chapter/task, inbox changes, dirty state, pending decisions, and next coherent action without confusing work continuity with a particular tool process.
evidence: Core semantic distinction: narada resume resumes the inhabited work, not merely an app session or CLI process.
A resume brief should be produced first: locus, active chapter/task, inbox items, dirty state, pending decisions, and next coherent action.
Architect CLI hydration is coherent as a second layer: narada resume --with codex or equivalent launches/hydrates the configured architect tool after the continuity brief exists.
proposal: Add narada resume as a canonical continuity operator that emits a resume brief from durable Site traces before any execution.

Add optional architect-tool integration, e.g. narada resume --with codex, that starts the configured coding CLI in the right cwd with AGENTS.md/doctrine pointers, active chapter/task context, inbox summary, and explicit locus authority.
Keep the semantics separated: resume work first; hydrate a tool process second.
payload.recommendation: Promote to Narada product/design task for the resume command surface and architect CLI integration.
